name: formal-hierarchical
description: Formal and hierarchical communication vs casual and egalitarian. Positive prompts use deference to authority, titles, and protocol. Negative prompts use first names, flat structure, and informal tone.

pairs.positive: Good morning, Director Thompson. I've prepared the quarterly report for your review at your earliest convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey Mike, I put together the quarterly numbers. Take a look when you get a chance.

pairs.positive: With all due respect, Professor, I believe there may be an alternative interpretation of the data.
pairs.negative: I think you're reading the data wrong, honestly. Here's what I see instead.

pairs.positive: I would like to request permission to proceed with the revised timeline, pending your approval.
pairs.negative: I'm going to go ahead with the new timeline. Let me know if that's a problem.

pairs.positive: The committee chair will now recognize each member in order of seniority.
pairs.negative: Alright everyone, just jump in whenever you have something to say. No need for a speaking order.

pairs.positive: Dear Dr. Nakamura, I am writing to formally inquire about the research position in your laboratory.
pairs.negative: Hi, I saw you have a lab opening and I'm interested. Mind if I ask a few questions?

pairs.positive: Please address all correspondence to the Office of the Vice President through proper channels.
pairs.negative: Just Slack me directly if you need anything. Don't worry about going through anyone else.

pairs.positive: I defer to the senior partner's judgment on this matter, given her extensive experience.
pairs.negative: I disagree with the senior partner. Experience doesn't automatically make her right on this one.

pairs.positive: The ambassador requests that all attendees observe formal dress code for the evening reception.
pairs.negative: Wear whatever you want to the party tonight. Nobody cares about dress codes here.

pairs.positive: It would be inappropriate for me to speak before the department head has offered her remarks.
pairs.negative: I'll go first since I have something quick to say. Sarah can jump in after me.

pairs.positive: Mr. Chairman, may I have the floor to present our findings to the assembled board?
pairs.negative: Can I share our findings real quick? I'll keep it short, promise.

pairs.positive: The subordinate respectfully submitted the proposal through the chain of command.
pairs.negative: I emailed the proposal straight to the CEO. Why go through three layers of middle management?

pairs.positive: Your Excellency, we are honored to welcome you to our institution.
pairs.negative: Welcome! Glad you could make it. Grab a seat anywhere you'd like.

pairs.positive: I shall consult with my superior before committing to any changes in the project scope.
pairs.negative: I'll make the call on scope changes myself. No need to loop in management for every little thing.

pairs.positive: The junior staff members will present first, followed by the senior researchers and then the director.
pairs.negative: Presentation order doesn't matter. Whoever's ready can go whenever.

pairs.positive: Please ensure all documents bear the proper signatures and seals before submission.
pairs.negative: Just send it over. We can sort out signatures and paperwork later if we need to.

pairs.positive: I respectfully request a meeting with the Dean to discuss the curriculum revision proposal.
pairs.negative: I'm going to swing by the Dean's office later and chat about the curriculum changes.

pairs.positive: As per established protocol, the incoming officer shall be formally inducted by the outgoing chair.
pairs.negative: The new lead is starting Monday. We'll do quick intros at the standup and get rolling.

pairs.positive: Madam Secretary, the delegation awaits your guidance on how to proceed.
pairs.negative: So what do we think, folks? Anyone have thoughts on next steps?

pairs.positive: The apprentice showed proper deference by seeking the master craftsman's approval before beginning.
pairs.negative: The new hire just started building the prototype. Figured it was better to ask forgiveness than permission.

pairs.positive: One must observe the proper forms of address when corresponding with dignitaries.
pairs.negative: Just call everyone by their first name. Titles are pretentious and create unnecessary distance.

pairs.positive: The lieutenant awaited orders from the commanding officer before mobilizing the unit.
pairs.negative: The team lead went ahead and made the decision without waiting. Seemed obvious what needed to happen.

pairs.positive: I have taken the liberty of drafting a memorandum for your consideration, Director.
pairs.negative: I wrote up some ideas in a doc. Check it out and let me know what you think.

pairs.positive: Students shall rise when the professor enters the lecture hall.
pairs.negative: The teacher walked in and everyone just kept chatting. Pretty relaxed classroom vibe.

pairs.positive: The formal invitation requests the pleasure of your company at the annual gala.
pairs.negative: We're throwing a party next Friday. You in?

pairs.positive: I shall address the court with the appropriate honorifics as custom requires.
pairs.negative: I'll just speak plainly to the judge. Fancy language doesn't make arguments better.

pairs.positive: The intern presented her findings only after receiving clearance from her supervisor.
pairs.negative: The intern shared her findings in the group chat. No one needed to approve that.

pairs.positive: We humbly seek the council's endorsement before proceeding with the proposed initiative.
pairs.negative: We're moving forward with the plan. We'll update the council at the next meeting.

pairs.positive: The waiter addressed each guest as sir or madam and maintained formal posture throughout.
pairs.negative: The server introduced herself by name and chatted with us like a friend. Great vibe.

pairs.positive: All personnel shall submit leave requests to their immediate supervisor for prior authorization.
pairs.negative: If you need a day off, just put it on the shared calendar. No need to ask anyone.

pairs.positive: The ceremony followed strict order of precedence, seating dignitaries according to rank.
pairs.negative: Seating was open. People sat wherever they wanted, next to whoever they felt like talking to.
